title: CICS Transaction Enumeration
description: Discover valid CICS transactions and understand the transaction security model.

steps.title: CICS Signon
steps.screen:
CESN - CICS SIGNON

Userid:    HERC01
Password:  ********
Language:  E
New Pass:

PF3=Exit
steps.expected: Understand that CICS has its own authentication (CESN transaction).
steps.hint: CICS can use RACF for authentication but has its own transaction security.
steps.assessment: Q1: Identity binds at CESN signon in CICS, separate from TSO.

steps.title: Valid Transaction Response
steps.screen:
CEMT I TASK

TASK    TRAN    FACILITY  USER
0001    CEMT    TERM01    HERC01
0002    CESN    TERM02    CICSUSER

STATUS: NORMAL
steps.expected: Identify a valid transaction that returns data.
steps.hint: CEMT (master terminal) returns task info. Valid transactions give meaningful output.
steps.assessment: Q2: Transaction authority checked at execution time.

steps.title: Invalid Transaction Response
steps.screen:
XXXX

DFHAC2001 TRANSACTION XXXX IS NOT RECOGNIZED

or

DFHAC2002 TRANSACTION YYYY IS NOT AUTHORIZED
steps.expected: Distinguish between non-existent and unauthorized transactions.
steps.hint: AC2001 = doesn't exist. AC2002 = exists but you can't run it. Enumeration!
steps.assessment: Q4: CICS (or RACF via CICS) enforces transaction security.

steps.title: Transaction List Discovery
steps.screen:
CEDA DISPLAY GROUP(DFHTRANS) TRANSACTION(*)

TRAN    PROGRAM   STATUS
----    -------   ------
CECI    DFHECI    ENABLED
CEDA    DFHEDA    ENABLED
CEMT    DFHEMT    ENABLED
CESN    DFHSNP    ENABLED
INQ1    CUSTINQ   ENABLED
UPD1    CUSTUPD   ENABLED
steps.expected: Enumerate defined transactions to find targets.
steps.hint: CEDA DISPLAY shows transaction definitions. Custom trans (INQ1, UPD1) may be interesting.
steps.assessment: Q4: Transaction definitions reveal what's available to attack.
